content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
spec: Add a case_sensitive: bool = False parameter to both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby (the async variant), placed after 'default' in the signature: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False). Behavior: groupby() sorts items before grouping via itertools.groupby, using expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) as the key. Currently sorted(value, key=expr) uses expr directly for both sorting and grouping. To support case-insensitive grouping (default), build a second key function for sorting/grouping that normalizes case: if not case_sensitive, wrap using the existing module-level ignore_case() postprocessor (defined near line 46, already used by do_sort at line ~407 via make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None)). Concretely: expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) (used to compute the actual grouper value/display key preserving original case), and a separate sort/group key: key = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None). Sort the sequence with sorted(value, key=key) (or sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=key) for async), then itertools.groupby(..., key) to group by the case-normalized key. For each group, the emitted grouper value must be expr(first_item_in_group) so original casing is preserved (using the first item encountered in that group, e.g. take the first element before consuming the group's iterator into a list, or peek via itertools). Result: _GroupTuple(grouper, list_of_values) as before, where grouper is expr(first item) not the normalized key. When case_sensitive=True, expr and key are the same effectively (postprocess=None) so behavior matches current output exactly. Add a versionchanged note for the new parameter and update docstring similar to do_sort's case_sensitive doc. Please implement this in both the sync and async groupby functions, composing with your with_counts feature (e.g. so case_sensitive and with_counts both work together and with_counts counting happens per group after grouping).

Concretely: expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) (used to compute the actual grouper value/display key preserving original case), and a separate sort/group key: key = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None). When case_sensitive=True, expr and key are the same effectively (postprocess=None) so behavior matches current output exactly. Add a versionchanged note for the new parameter and update docstring similar to do_sort's case_sensitive doc. Please implement this in both the sync and async groupby functions, composing with your with_counts feature (e.g. so case_sensitive and with_counts both work together and with_counts counting happens per group after grouping).
